free_text: Uranium, neptunium, americium oxides and fluoride
composition of thorium applied to the backings of
aluminium oxide Al2O3 115 mg/cm2 thick using vacuum
semiconductor silicon detectors of surface-barrier type
DKPS-100, placed at 150 mm distance from the chamber
centre. The monitor of tritons scattered by the target
was placed at the angle of (20.3+0.3)degr. to the
triton beam direction and had the solid angle
Two series of measurements were performed. In the first
series there was employed one fission fragment detector
located at the angle of 130 degr. as related to the
direction of the beam of accelerated tritons.
In the second series there was measured the angular
distribution of fission fragments with simultaneous use
of 4 detectors. One of them was fixed in position at
the angle of 90 degr., while the other three were
placed on a rotating disk in such a way that there was
the possibility to install each of them within the
angle range from 25 to 175 degr. as related to the
The cross section was calculated from formula:

determined through the change transferred by the triton
beam to the target. The change was measured by the
